title: list: concurrent list.sort() of a shared list races (binarysort rewrites the detached array with no critical section)
description: list_sort_impl takes NO per-object critical section: it detaches the items array (ob_item=NULL, size=0) and, with no key func, sorts saved_ob_item (the list's own array) in place via binarysort. Two threads sorting the same shared list (or a concurrent reader holding the pre-detach ob_item) race on the array slots. RULED A BUG by Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s) 2026-07-15 (same shared-builtin class).
sites: Objects/listobject.c binarysort:1918 (a[L] = pivot -- rewrites the detached items array)
Objects/listobject.c list_sort_impl:2968-2977 (saves+detaches ob_item, no critical section; lo.keys = saved_ob_item)
race_kind: mutate-while-mutate on a shared builtin list (concurrent sort, no critical section)
shared_state: the shared list's items array (saved_ob_item, sorted in place)
severity: low observed (value-benign, crash-free), but an unsynchronized in-place rewrite of a shared list's array
repro_reliability: CLEAN plain-list synthetic (repro.py): 8/8 (100%) exit-66, deterministic. Isolates binarysort-vs-read with a barrier -- re-scramble the shared list while readers are PARKED (no race), then release readers to iterate WHILE the sorter runs list.sort(), so list_sort_impl's in-place binarysort writes (listobject.c:1918) race the iterator's lock-free item read (_PyList_GetItemRefNoLock / _Py_TryXGetRef -> _Py_atomic_load_ptr). Supersedes the earlier shrinkray-minimized email._header_value_parser.Comment vehicle (list subclass, ~15-30%/run).
suggested_fix: take the list's per-object critical section in list_sort_impl (or otherwise synchronize the detach+in-place-sort against concurrent readers/sorters), consistent with the other list mutators.
ruling: Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s, CPython RM), 2026-07-15: the shared-builtin concurrent-access class 'is a bug'; concurrent list.sort() falls under it.
notes: Formerly HELD as a dev-question ('is concurrent list.sort() on a shared list intended to stay crash-safe?'); the Yhg1s ruling answers it (yes, it's a bug). Now REPRODUCED in isolation via shrinkray-minimized repro.py (the earlier sort-vs-sort attempt failed; the real trigger is sort-vs-read). If filed, note the detach-without-lock mechanism explicitly. API-SEARCH 2026-07-17: adjacent to the CLOSED list-race class #129069 and governed by the merged #142519 contract; sort-vs-read residual, not a novel discovery. CLEAN REPRO 2026-07-17: replaced the email-parser vehicle with a plain-list synthetic that reproduces 100% (8/8). The trick is a barrier that confines the list re-scramble (a write) to a readers-parked phase, so only sort-vs-read overlaps in the active phase -- otherwise the re-scramble write races readers (TSAN-0013 class) and halt_on_error stops on that instead. list.sort() with NO key= sorts the list's own ob_item in place, which is what makes binarysort the writer.
mechanism: email._header_value_parser.Comment subclasses list; sharing one across threads and hammering its methods runs list.sort() (writer: binarysort rewrites the detached array in place, no critical section) concurrently with readers that iterate the list (list_get_item_ref/_Py_TryXGetRef). sort-vs-READ, not sort-vs-sort -- the reader holds ob_item across slot reads while the in-place sort rewrites, a wider window. (A plain sort-vs-sort loop does NOT reproduce.)
minimized_with: shrinkray (interestingness = 'in binarysort' under the TSan build); 32.9 kB -> 867 B / 28 lines

related_issues.note: the list mutation-vs-read race class (CLOSED, fix #131882 covered extend/slice-assign/in-place-mult memcpy writes). Concurrent list.sort()'s in-place binarysort rewrite vs a concurrent reader is an ADJACENT operation that fix did not cover; same class.

MERGED list thread-safety docs: lock-free readers 'may return results affected by concurrent modifications' and use atomic item reads; 'all other operations block using the per-object lock'. list.sort holds the per-object lock, so the defect is that a concurrent lock-free reader's plain load races sort's in-place writes -- the reader-atomicity gap again.
